articles.title: चंडीगढ़ में सड़कें खाली और बाजारों में भी सन्नाटा; अगले छह दिन रहें अलर्ट, चेतावनी को न करें नजरअंदाज - Jagran
articles.search_term: ("हीट एक्शन प्लान" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR चेतावनी OR "रेड अलर्ट" OR "ऑरेंज अलर्ट" OR "येलो अलर्ट" OR एडवाइजरी OR "राहत सामग्री" OR "राहत शिविर" OR मुआवजा) Chandigarh
articles.full_text:
चंडीगढ़ में सड़कें खाली और बाजारों में भी सन्नाटा; अगले छह दिन रहें अलर्ट, चेतावनी को न करें नजरअंदाज
चंडीगढ़ में 42.1 डिग्री सेल्सियस तापमान के साथ भीषण गर्मी और लू का प्रकोप जारी है, जिससे सड़कें और बाजार सूने पड़े हैं। मौसम विभाग ने अगले छह दिनों के ...और पढ़ें
HighLights
चंडीगढ़ में 42.1 डिग्री सेल्सियस पहुंचा तापमान।
अगले छह दिनों के लिए हीटवेव का अलर्ट जारी।
डिहाइड्रेशन और हीट स्ट्रोक के मामले बढ़ रहे।
जागरण संवाददाता, चंडीगढ़। चंडीगढ़ समेत ट्राईसिटी में मंगलवार को सड़कों पर आवाजाही काफी कम दिखी। बाजारों में भी सन्नाटा रहा। पर्यटक स्थलों पर भी लोगों की संख्या घट गई है। इसका कारण है कि भीषण गर्मी और लू के चलते लोग घरों से बाहर नहीं निकल रहे।
मौसम विभाग ने अगले छह दिनों के लिए हीटवेव का अलर्ट जारी किया है। स्वास्थ्य विशेषज्ञों ने लोगों को सतर्क रहने की सलाह देते हुए कहा है कि लापरवाही बरतने पर हीट स्ट्रोक और डिहाइड्रेशन का खतरा बढ़ सकता है।
मंगलवार को चंडीगढ़ में अधिकतम तापमान 42.1 डिग्री और न्यूनतम तापमान 24.8 डिग्री सेल्सियस दर्ज किया गया। स्वास्थ्य विशेषज्ञों के अनुसार भीषण गर्मी का असर केवल बुजुर्गों और बीमार लोगों तक सीमित नहीं है, बल्कि युवा और स्वस्थ लोग भी इसकी चपेट में आ रहे हैं।
अस्पतालों और स्वास्थ्य केंद्रों में चक्कर आने, उल्टी, बुखार, शरीर में पानी की कमी और त्वचा झुलसने की शिकायतों वाले मरीजों की संख्या बढ़ रही है। डाॅक्टरों का कहना है कि लू से बचाव के लिए दोपहर 12 बजे से शाम चार बजे तक धूप में निकलने से बचना चाहिए। पर्याप्त मात्रा में पानी पीना, हल्के रंग के सूती कपड़े पहनना और बाहर निकलते समय सिर को ढकना जरूरी है।
खबरें और भी
विशेषज्ञों ने चेतावनी दी है कि अत्यधिक गर्मी को हल्के में लेना खतरनाक साबित हो सकता है। मौसम विभाग के अनुसार फिलहाल तापमान में किसी बड़ी गिरावट की संभावना नहीं है। ऐसे में आने वाले दिनों में भी लोगों को गर्मी और लू के तीखे तेवर झेलने पड़ सकते हैं।

articles.title: rain provides relief from heatwave in delhi and north india - The News Mill
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Chandigarh
articles.full_text:
After enduring several days of intense heatwave conditions, parts of Delhi experienced rainfall on Tuesday evening, offering much-needed relief from the high temperatures.
The change in weather occurred amid ongoing heatwave conditions affecting the Delhi-NCR region in recent days.
Earlier, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasted that broader rainfall activity was expected to extend to Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, and various areas of Uttar Pradesh and Rajasthan from June 10 to 14 as a changing weather pattern approaches North India.
This system is expected to bring a transition to the weather across much of North India. The IMD also predicted isolated to scattered rainfall across Jammu and Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand from June 9 to 14.
Maximum temperatures in North India are projected to rise by 2-4 degrees Celsius until June 11, followed by a gradual decrease of 3-5 degrees Celsius, according to the IMD.
Currently, heatwave conditions persist in isolated pockets of Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Gilgit-Baltistan, Muzaffarabad, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, West Rajasthan, and Uttar Pradesh, with warnings in place until June 11.
The IMD indicated that a fresh Western Disturbance is likely to affect the Western Himalayan region starting June 11, bringing widespread changes in weather patterns.
This shift is expected to include severe weather events. The IMD warned of thunder squalls with wind speeds reaching 50-60 kmph, along with gusty winds and lightning across Rajasthan, Haryana, Delhi, and Uttar Pradesh between June 11 and 12.
Isolated hailstorms are anticipated in Himachal Pradesh, Jammu and Kashmir, and Uttarakhand during June 11-12, with similar risks forecast for Haryana, Chandigarh, and Delhi on June 12.
Residents are advised to monitor local weather updates closely and be prepared to relocate to safer areas if conditions deteriorate.

articles.title: Bathinda zoo implements measures to protect animals from heatwave - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
The Bathinda Zoo has introduced special arrangements to safeguard animals from rising temperatures as the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued a five-day yellow alert for heatwave conditions in Punjab and Haryana starting Monday, an official confirmed on Sunday.
Dr Vijay Kumar, Assistant Director of the Bathinda Animal Husbandry Department, stated, “Our department is responsible for the health services of the animals. We have issued advisories regarding the adverse effects of heatwaves. The Forest Department strictly follows all protocols, including quarantine procedures for new animals to acclimate them to the environment before integrating them into the general wards. The constant access to water and that feeding times are in the morning and evening to conserve energy during hot weather. Veterinary officers are stationed at the zoo to ensure the safety and timely treatment of sick wildlife.”
The IMD forecasted heatwave conditions over Punjab and Haryana from May 18 to 22, with mainly dry weather expected to continue across Punjab, Haryana, and Chandigarh for the next five to six days. A gradual rise in maximum temperatures by about 4-5°C is predicted, leading to isolated heatwave conditions over southern and south-western parts of Punjab and Haryana. Maximum temperatures in northern and eastern parts of Punjab, Haryana, and Chandigarh are also expected to remain above normal during this period.
The highest maximum temperature recorded in Punjab was 41.0°C at Bathinda, although temperatures across the state are currently below normal with departures ranging from -1.6 to -3.0°C. In Haryana, Rohtak recorded the highest temperature at 40.8°C, with temperatures remaining normal.
The IMD advisory noted that moderate temperatures are tolerable for the general public but present moderate health concerns for vulnerable groups such as infants, the elderly, and individuals with chronic diseases. The public has been advised to avoid prolonged sun exposure during peak hours, stay hydrated, wear lightweight, light-coloured loose cotton clothing, and cover heads with cloth, hats, or umbrellas.
Regarding agriculture, the IMD recommended irrigation during the evening or early morning to prevent heat stress in crops.

articles.title: Chandigarh Weather Forecast for Tomorrow, Wednesday, 10 June 2026: Expect Clear Skies and a High of 39°C
articles.full_text:
Chandigarh Weather Forecast for Tomorrow, Wednesday, 10 June 2026: Expect Clear Skies and a High of 39°C
Chandigarh will experience hot and clear weather on Wednesday, June 10, with temperatures soaring to a high of 39 degrees Celsius. Residents are advised to stay hydrated, wear light cotton clothing, and avoid prolonged exposure to direct sunlight, especially during the peak afternoon hours. Scroll below to know more.
Chandigarh residents can expect a hot and sunny day tomorrow, Wednesday, June 10, 2026, as the city is forecast to experience clear skies throughout the day. The maximum temperature is expected to reach a scorching 39°C, while the low will hover around a comfortable 25°C. With a minimal 2% chance of rain, residents should prepare for continued dry conditions.
Chandigarh, Chandigarh Weather Live Tracker Map on Windy
The hourly outlook suggests a gradual increase in temperature from the early morning hours. Starting at 27°C at midnight, the mercury will slowly climb, reaching 28°C by 6 AM. By 9 AM, temperatures will already be in the mid-30s, hitting 36°C, and will continue to rise to a peak of 39°C in the afternoon, likely around 3 PM. The wind will remain relatively light, with a maximum speed of 16 km/h, primarily observed in the morning hours and dropping to near calm by the evening.
Recent weather reports indicate a return of heat to the region, with temperatures previously breaching the 40°C mark. While tomorrow's forecast doesn't predict a return to such extreme highs, the sustained warmth necessitates caution. Residents are advised to stay hydrated, wear light cotton clothing, and avoid prolonged exposure to direct sunlight, especially during the peak afternoon hours. Hyderabad Weather Forecast & Update for Today, Tuesday, 09 June 2026: Expect Light Drizzle With High of 27°C.
For those with outdoor plans, including attending any local events or commuting, the clear skies and lack of rain are favourable. However, the intense heat will be a significant factor. It is advisable to carry water bottles and seek shaded areas whenever possible to mitigate the risk of heat-related discomfort. The prevailing weather conditions are conducive to outdoor activities, provided adequate precautions against the heat are taken.
